title: 1.1: Tests that document.content is asserted against an explicit expected text value from the consumed input (e.g., assertEqual/ assertIn with a known expected string/snippet)

1.2: In the end-to-end lifecycle test (the one that asserts parsed content and that the original input file is removed), the test includes an assertion on document.filename (e.g., assertEqual/assertIsNotNone/assertTrue on document.filename)

1.3: In the end-to-end lifecycle test (the one that asserts parsed content and that the original input file is removed), the test includes an assertion on document.archive_filename (e.g., assertEqual/assertIsNotNone/assertTrue on document.archive_filename)
1.5: In a baseline consumption test where no override_correspondent_id is provided, the test asserts document.correspondent is None after successful processing
1.6: In a baseline consumption test where no override_document_type_id is provided, the test asserts document.document_type is None after successful processing
1.7: Tests that the source file exists at document.source_path after document processing
1.8: Tests that a thumbnail file is created at document.thumbnail_path after document processing
1.9: Tests that an archive version file is created at document.archive_path after document processing
1.10: Tests that the original input file is deleted after successful document consumption
2.1: Places the test for document consumption workflow in src/documents/tests/test_consumer.py
3.1: All tests use Python unittest framework with TestCase class and self.assert* methods
3.2: All tests follow the test naming pattern testCamelCase
3.3: All tests use the Arrange-Act-Assert structure with setup of test file, consumer method call, and multiple assertions
4.1: Uses @override_settings decorator to configure PAPERLESS_FILENAME_FORMAT for the document consumption test
4.2: Uses self.consumer.try_consume_file() method to process the test document
4.3: Uses self.get_test_file() helper method to obtain the input test file for consumption
